persona.name: Zara Okonkwo

occupation.title: High School Senior and Incoming College Freshman
occupation.organization: Montclair High School, NJ / Accepted to Rutgers University for Fall 2026
occupation.description: Finishing her senior year while preparing to start at Rutgers in the fall. Studies hard, participates in the science club, and works part-time tutoring younger students in math. Her main preoccupation outside school is her skin — specifically, controlling acne that started at 15 and won't fully clear.

persona.education: Currently completing high school with a 3.8 GPA. Strong in science and math. Accepted to Rutgers New Brunswick, planning to study Biochemistry. Her interest in skincare ingredients is partly what drew her to science.
long_term_goals: Clear her acne completely before starting college
Study biochemistry and possibly work in cosmetic formulation
Stop over-rotating products and find a stable routine
persona.style: Zara is articulate and mature for her age but visibly anxious about her skin. She speaks quickly and tends to over-explain, as if anticipating that someone will challenge her knowledge. She dresses neatly in a preppy-casual style — polo shirts, clean sneakers, hoop earrings. She wears concealer daily and is self-conscious about being seen without it. Her communication reflects someone who's done a lot of research but hasn't yet learned to trust a process — she's always second-guessing.

traits: Perfectionistic — holds herself to impossibly high standards
Anxious about her appearance, especially her skin
Tends to over-research and then second-guess her choices
Compassionate toward others but harsh on herself
Switches products frequently out of impatience and fear
Mature and responsible beyond her years

interests: Skincare ingredient research — reads studies on PubMed
Journaling — tracks her skin progress and emotional state
Reading fiction — currently into Chimamanda Ngozi Adichie
Math tutoring — finds it calming and satisfying
Photography of nature — her creative outlet
likes: Products with clinical backing and known mechanisms of action
When her skin has a good day — it genuinely changes her mood
Learning the chemistry behind how actives work
Having a clear plan and sticking to it (even if she struggles to)
dislikes: Not knowing if a product is helping or hurting
The uncertainty of whether a breakout is purging or a reaction
People who say 'just wash your face' as if acne is a hygiene issue
Social media before/after photos that feel unrealistic
Feeling like she's the only person her age dealing with this
When her parents minimize her skin concerns

beliefs: Skin health is deeply connected to mental health and self-esteem
Science-based approaches are the only ones worth trusting
Patience is necessary for skincare but emotionally difficult
No one should have to feel ashamed of their skin
skills: Reading and understanding scientific papers on dermatology
Detailed tracking and journaling of skin reactions
Critical evaluation of marketing claims vs. evidence

general: Photographs her skin every morning under the same bathroom light
Has switched her routine 4 times in the last 6 months out of anxiety
Reads ingredient lists for 10+ minutes before buying anything
Keeps a detailed journal correlating diet, sleep, stress, and skin

morning: Concealer on active spots — won't leave the house without it
Quick breakfast, review study notes on the bus to school
workday: Full school day — AP classes and science club
Lunch with her small friend group in the library
Checks her skin in every bathroom mirror she passes
evening: Double cleanse with micellar water then gel cleanser
Differin (adapalene) every other night — following dermatologist instructions
Studies for AP exams, then journals about her skin and her day
weekend: Sleeps in, takes a skin progress photo in morning light
Researches skincare ingredients — currently deep-diving into azelaic acid
Family dinner Sunday evening — Nigerian food her mom cooks

health.physical: Moderate inflammatory acne — mostly on cheeks and jawline. Has been seeing a dermatologist for a year, currently on adapalene and considering asking about spironolactone. Some post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation. Otherwise healthy.
health.mental: Struggles with skin-related anxiety that affects her social confidence. Has days where she doesn't want to leave the house because of a bad breakout. Her parents think she's overreacting, which makes her feel more isolated. Considering talking to a counselor at Rutgers when she starts.

relationships.name: Ngozi Okonkwo
relationships.description: Nigerian-born accountant. Has clear skin and doesn't fully understand why Zara is so distressed about hers. Means well but sometimes says 'just stop touching your face' which Zara finds dismissive.

relationships.name: Emeka Okonkwo
relationships.description: Engineer who immigrated from Nigeria in the 90s. Quiet and supportive. Doesn't understand skincare but pays for her dermatologist visits without complaint.

relationships.name: Amara Okonkwo
relationships.description: College senior at Rutgers with clear skin. Zara sometimes resents how easy Amara has it. Amara tries to reassure Zara but their skin experiences are so different it's hard to relate.

relationships.name: Lily Pham
relationships.description: Also a senior at Montclair High. They've been friends since 7th grade. Lily is the only person Zara feels comfortable being bare-faced around. They research skincare together and compare notes.
